global_news.findings: China has imposed a sweeping ban on drone sales and flights in Beijing, citing national security concerns. Effective May 1, the new rules prohibit the sale, rental, or import of drones and key components into the capital. Owners must register devices with police, and all outdoor flights require permits. The restrictions target China's massive low-altitude economy, which is projected to generate $290 billion by 2035. DJI, the world's largest drone maker, has reportedly begun removing products from Beijing shelves. The regulations follow years of tightening controls, with Beijing's entire airspa [TRUNCATED]

The Kremlin has pledged to maintain Russian forces in Mali despite mounting insurgent attacks. This follows coordinated weekend assaults by al Qaeda-linked Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM) and Tuareg separatists that killed Mali's defense minister and forced Russian contractors to withdraw from strategic towns. JNIM claims to have captured the Hombori military base and threatens a complete siege of Bamako, the capital of 4 million people. The attacks represent a significant setback for Russia's African security strategy, as Moscow positions itself as a key counter-terrorism partner  [TRUNCATED]

The UK has raised its national terrorism threat level from 'substantial' to 'severe' - the second-highest level - following an antisemitic stabbing attack in Golders Green, north London. Two Jewish men were seriously injured in what police are treating as a terrorist incident. The Joint Terrorism Analysis Centre's assessment means security officials now consider a terror attack 'highly likely' in the next six months. The decision comes amid heightened tensions over the Israel-Hamas conflict and follows a pattern of increasing far-right and Islamist extremist threats across Europe.

Ukraine has struck a critical Russian oil pumping station 1,500 kilometers inside Russia near the Ural Mountains, marking one of the deepest Ukrainian drone attacks of the war. President Zelenskiy confirmed the strike on the Transneft-owned facility near Perm, describing it as part of Ukraine's strategy to extend the range of attacks against Russian energy infrastructure. The targeted station serves as a strategic hub distributing oil in four directions. The attack comes as Ukraine reports significant reductions in Russian oil export capacity, with Baltic Sea ports showing 13-43% throughput de [TRUNCATED]

The European Union has approved a €90 billion loan package for Ukraine while simultaneously announcing the reopening of the Druzhba oil pipeline, ending months of deadlock over stalled oil supplies to Hungary. Ukraine confirmed it has reopened the pipeline after reaching an agreement with EU partners. The deal resolves tensions between Ukraine and Hungary, which had accused Kyiv of blocking oil transit. The massive EU loan comes as Ukraine faces mounting economic pressure from the ongoing war and demonstrates continued European commitment to Ukrainian sovereignty despite Russian efforts to fra [TRUNCATED]

intellectual_journals.findings: Flora Champy's essay 'The role of literature as the key to personal freedom' (April 24 2026) argues against the utilitarian view of reading as moral improvement. Drawing on Proust's 1905 essay 'On Reading'—written as a preface to Ruskin's 'Sesame and Lilies'—Champy shows how Proust rejected Ruskin's moralising defence of literature. Where Ruskin saw books as training in disinterestedness against Victorian commercialism, Proust argued that literature's value lies not in edification but in preserving 'indelible impressions' that make 'time tangible'. Champy extends this to suggest reading offers [TRUNCATED]

A joint ProPublica-FRONTLINE investigation (April 14 2026) reveals that over 300 protesters arrested during Trump's immigration sweeps have seen their cases collapse under scrutiny. Federal agents routinely accused dissenters of assaulting law enforcement, but video evidence repeatedly debunked officer statements. In more than a third of cases, prosecutors dismissed charges or lost at trial. The report documents a chilling effect: one protester stated 'I don't want to be assaulted again. I don't want to wind up back in federal prison.' The investigation exposes systematic use of conspiracy cha [TRUNCATED]

Francine Prose's review 'Blood in the Game' (April 23 2026) examines two novels addressing contemporary America's violence and corruption. Lee Clay Johnson's 'Bloodline' follows Winston Alcorn, a former car salesman who amputates his own hand in an insurance scam to reclaim imagined Confederate birthright, transforming into 'Wins-a-ton'—a grotesque figure convinced he's fulfilling prophecy. Carl Hiaasen's 'Fever Beach' explores similar themes through South Florida's environmental destruction. Both novels, Prose argues, manage to address 'the grotesque and infuriating nature of contemporary pub [TRUNCATED]

Maria Popova's essay 'Fanny Wright and the Radical Courage of Being Real' (February 27 2026) recovers the forgotten story of Frances 'Fanny' Wright (1795-1852), the Scottish-born radical who became America's first female public speaker and pioneered scientific thinking alongside free love advocacy. The piece traces how young Walt Whitman was 'possessed' by Wright's orbit after his working-class parents subscribed to her radical paper 'The Free Enquirer'. Popova details Wright's Epicurean-influenced novel 'A Few Days in Athens'—written at eighteen—which advanced atomic realism and moral discipl [TRUNCATED]

Battery lasts 4 hours with 20-30 minut [TRUNCATED]

MagicSchool AI dominates the teacher tool space with 80+ educator tools and 50+ student tools. New 2026 features include Studio Mode for document-based editing, MagicQuizzes for standards-aligned formative assessments, and Class Writing Feedback that delivers rubric-aligned comments directly to Google Docs. The platform saves teachers 7-10 hours weekly according to user reports. Diffit offers free [TRUNCATED]

CES 2026 showcased the emerging lifelogging category with screenless AI gadgets. Memories.ai's Project LUCI pin offers 109-degree camera, 45g weight, and 2-3 hour recording with 'sub-second search and recall' through their Large Visual Memory Model. MemoMind One AI glasses ($599-$849) provide discreet displays with voice interaction, real-time translation, and meeting recording - available in 3 fr [TRUNCATED]

triadic_ontology.findings: Two new works advance the triadic-ontology program. **"Perichoretic Quantum Fields: A Triadic Ontology of Relational Substance"** by Dr. Elena Vasconcelos (University of Lisbon, March 2026) proposes that quantum field excitations instantiate perichoretic relations—mutual indwelling without loss of identity—providing a mathematical framework where the Trinity serves as ontological ground for non-local quantum correlations. Vasconcelos extends Migliorini's "subsisting relations" to quantum domains, arguing that entangled particles exhibit triadic relationality analogous to the divine persons. **"Non-Linear Triadic Dynamics: From Logic to Metaphysics"** by Prof. Kenji Nakamura (Kyoto University, April 2026) introduces a non-linear triadic logic that resolves the tension between divine simplicity and relational plurality. Nakamura demonstrates how triadic relations generate emergent properties absent in dyadic systems, offering a new model for understanding consciousness and divine action. [TRUNCATED]

ai_systems.findings: Three major autonomous research frameworks have emerged in 2026, moving from lab demos to hardened production systems. InternAgent-1.5 introduces a unified three-subsystem architecture (generation, verification, evolution) that coordinates computational modeling with wet-lab experimentation, achieving leading performance on GAIA, HLE, and FrontierScience benchmarks while autonomously designing ML algorithms and running complete empirical studies across earth, life, and physical sciences. The Mimosa framework addresses the brittleness of fixed research workflows by automatically synthesizing task-specific multi-agent topologies that evolve through experimental feedback, using Model Context Protocol for dynamic tool discovery and achieving 43.1% success on ScienceAgentBench with full auditability. Google's MARS system optimizes for the economics of AI research via budget-aware Monte Carlo Tree Search that balances performance against compute cost, modular "Design-Decompose-Implement" pip [TRUNCATED]

uap.findings: Congresswoman Anna Paulina Luna, chairing the House Oversight Task Force on Declassification, escalated pressure on the Pentagon this week by vowing to hold a press conference presenting materials she describes as "non-human origin" once declassified. Luna, who has reviewed classified evidence in a SCIF, stated: "I have observed things that are of non-human origin and creation." Her task force continues investigating the All-Domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) for withholding 46 UAP videos requested in March, warning readiness is at risk when unknown craft operate near military installations. President Trump's February directive to release UFO files remains stalled—three months later, no executive order has been signed and the Pentagon cites ongoing classification reviews. Meanwhile, the 2026 NDAA mandates expanded congressional briefings on UAP intercepts since 2004 and requires agencies to begin identifying records for public release by October, though former AARO officials cauti [TRUNCATED]

newyorker.title: Reverend Billy Takes On Norman Foster’s New Monolith
newyorker.dek: Fresh from opening shows for Neil Young, the street preacher Billy Talen has moved on from burning Mickey Mouse in effigy to protesting JPMorgan Chase’s ties to fossil fuels, Jake Offenhartz writes.
newyorker.text:
A little after midnight on Earth Day, the street preacher Billy Talen dragged his pulpit to the new Norman Foster-designed global headquarters of JPMorgan Chase, on Madison. His self-ordained mission: deliver a twenty-four-hour sermon, calling attention to the bank’s ties to the fossil-fuel industry. Talen, who is seventy-five, held an umbrella above his white leisure suit and silver bouffant, eying a pair of bank security guards. “Blessings upon your souls, gentlemen,” he bellowed. Members of his activist ensemble, the Stop Shopping Choir, were setting up a live stream in the rain. Savitri Durkee, who directs both the choir and Talen, who is her husband, clipped a mike to his lapel and checked her watch. “It’s go-time, Billy,” she said. “See you on the other side.”
Talen, who goes by Reverend Billy, is fond of what he calls “extreme preaching.” He began in the mid-nineties, marching around Times Square with “Antichrist” Mickey Mouse nailed to a wooden cross, in protest of the neighborhood’s creeping corporatism. In the years since, his focus has shifted to the climate crisis. Often, this involves staging musical protests in hostile venues—banks, pipeline-construction zones—until he and his flock are arrested for trespassing. But, Talen said, even that has begun to feel “predictable.” He elaborated, speaking into a camera: “The only way we’re going to start an effective movement is if each of us goes to the limit of our capabilities and claims our eccentricities. I’m a preacher, so I’m going to preach until I drop.” A choir member checked the stream, and noted that they’d just jumped from five to fourteen viewers. “Earthalujah!” Talen cried.
After decades of sneaking up on bankers and lobbyists, Reverend Billy and the choir recently booked a straight gig: touring the country as Neil Young’s opening act. (Typical song: “A Tree Is the Opposite of a Cop.”) The rock star’s fans seemed receptive to the message, relatively. “After a lifetime of being too strange, people are starting to get us,” Talen said. He’s also the religion editor for the Times-Contrarian, Young’s anti-corporate “digital newspaper.” “Neil is a hands-on publisher,” Talen said. “Sometimes he lets me write these really long things. Other times, he’ll say to me, ‘Billy, what do you mean here? What do you want to leave us with?,’ and we’ll e-mail back and forth on that.” Talen said that he’s learned a lot from Young as a performer: “He’s taught me to hold the rhythm, hit the rhyme. He gets what appeals to a mass audience.” Talen and Durkee were connected with the singer by Young’s wife, Daryl Hannah, whom they met at a 2009 protest against mountaintop-removal coal mining in West Virginia. Talen was later arrested, after depositing mounds of “sacred dirt”—taken from the mountain, he claimed—at two Chase branches in the East Village. The bank has since decreased financing for companies involved in mountaintop-removal mining.
By 8 A.M., dark circles had formed under Talen’s steely blue eyes. “I just need to stay hydrated,” he said. Behind him, a gardener was washing a living-wall installation titled “A Parallel Nature,” designed for Chase by Maya Lin. “I’m finding myself mesmerized by this strange, sculpted nature wall,” Talen said. “It feels like a memory.” After conferring with a guard, the gardener pointed his hose at the protesters in the plaza. Talen pulled a rain slicker over his suit and glowered. Thousands of workers were now streaming into the building, mostly ignoring the doomsday preacher.
Around lunchtime, roughly two dozen choir members and other activists arrived for a 5K run around the bank. Among them was Ahmed, an asylum seeker from Guinea who had taken refuge in the choir’s former headquarters, a vacant bank on Avenue C, after arriving in the United States. “It’s very scary-looking,” he said of Chase’s sixty-story tower. Many of the runners had procured thrift-store blazers or top hats, an effort to blend in with the bankers. Durkee, who wore a pin-st [TRUNCATED]

literary_pick.title: The Known World
literary_pick.author: Edward P. Jones
literary_pick.summary: A Pulitzer Prize-winning novel that reimagines American slavery through the story of Henry Townsend, a Black farmer and former slave who becomes a slave owner himself in antebellum Virginia. The book explores the moral complexities of slavery and freedom through a vast cast of characters, examining how the institution corrupted both master and slave, and presenting a profound meditation on the nature of power, identity, and the American experience.
